labels.name: Needs Reproduction
labels.description: Reproducible steps needed

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Description The web app is crashing intermittently with no errors visible in the JS console after the crash occurs. This makes it difficult to debug since the console state is lost on crash. Most likely this crash https://expensify.sentry.io/issues/APP-2BN ## Steps to reproduce Unknown — needs reproduction. The reporter experienced multiple crashes on the web app but was not able to capture console output at the time of the crash. ## Expected behavior The web app should not crash unexpectedly. ## Actual behavior The web app crashes intermittently. No JS console errors are visible after the crash (likely because the page reloads and console is cleared). ## Additional context - Consider leveraging Sentry for better crash reporting so we don't rely on users having the console open - **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1778782686789009

title: [$250] Bulk expense edit hangs and crashes on large reports

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem When a report has many expenses (40-50+), if you bulk edit them (e.g. toggle billable to false for all), the UI hangs noticeably and the browser tab can become unresponsive. ## Solution Two changes needed here: - The bulk edit flow makes an unnecessary report reload after each individual expense update, causing a flood of operations. This call should be removed. - The save button should show a loading spinner while the edits are processing so users know something's happening and don't keep clicking.

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** V9 3.74 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/86177 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** dreamnitethedragon+vegst@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Redmi note 10s android 13 **App Component:** Workspace Settings ## Action Performed: 1. Launch app 2. Open workspace chat 3. Create a manual expense with category and merchant 4.Open the expense and split the expense 5. Open the split expense- amount - category - plus sign - add a category "uncategorized" 6. Save the split 7. Note missing category shown & system message "changed the category to "Uncategorized" (previously "Fees")" 8. Open the category and select uncategorized category from list 9. Note missing category is shown & system message shows "changed the expense. 10. Tap category - plus - add uncategorized category again 11. Note category name already exists error shown ## Expected Result: Uncategorized category should behave consistently across all flows. If selected successfully, it should remain visible in category row. ## Actual Result: Selected Uncategorized category is not displayed in category row. System messages indicate category change succeeded. App prevents recreating Uncategorized because it already exists. Category handling behaves inconsistently across flows. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b2fda6dc-c869-4b0c-98bd-963aeb1c468b [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Connecting as delegate does not change the pusher subscription of the user channel to the new accountID

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Coming from https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/636456 # Problem We are noticing that after switching to a delegate account, you don't get any pusher messages for the account you delegated into. Sometimes, we also see `AuthenticatePusher` calls using the accountID of the original account, which fails with a 403 error. This also means that we see GetMissingOnyxMessages calls every time there's a new update and you do an API call, because the messages were not delivered to the app because it is subscribed to the wrong channel. # Solution It seems that we are not properly unsubscribing from the original account pusher channel and resubscribing in the new account channel or maybe we are and [this](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/1b89b63083d7ca1981db71f3ac0023441881967c/src/libs/Pusher/index.ts#L70-L73) prevents it from doing what it should. More investigation is needed

Navigate to staging.expensify.com (OD) 2. Select "Manage my company's expenses (10+)" 3. Sign up with a account with the following format: [anything]@tstsg.com 4. Click on "Get Started" and complete the signup process 5. When redirected to NewDot, select 51-100 - QBO - complete the onboarding 6. In the #admins room observe the setup specialist email and avatar ## Expected Result: Setup specialist is displayed as a qa.guide@team.expensify.com and has a non-fallback avatar ## Actual Result: Setup specialist is displayed as a just onboarded user's email with a fallback avatar ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6fafcacd-5316-432a-9679-ef4d540d1ae2 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** V9.3.74 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-28774/folder/13176679/106914447/1413061850 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** applausetester+s1@applause.expensifail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Redmi note 10s android 13 **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: Pre-condition Payment currency set as USD for user A & B 1. Login new expensifail account - A 2. Login gmail account - user B 1. Launch app 2. As user B , send an IOU in USD 3. Open user A account 4. Turn to airoplane mode 5. Tap pay - pay with business account 6. Navigate to LHN & open workspace chat 7. Note IOU is shown in preview 8. Open the expense 9. Note total is shown as loading and no expense details displayed ## Expected Result: Expense report must be shown and expense details must be displayed. ## Actual Result: IOU is shown in preview & total is shown as loading,no expense details displayed in offline. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ee69aad-f01f-4a7a-b00b-4a3cb5837bda [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Re-entered odometer readings/image do not persist after refreshing the page

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.74-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-28774/folder/18717689/106413858/1413061849 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** kiatest.80+ck513262@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Windows 11/Chrome, Pixel 8a/Android 16 **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: Preconditions 1. User is logged in to NewDot 2. User is a Admin/member of at least one workspace Steps: 1/ Navigate to staging.new.expensify.com or launch Hybrid app 2. Click the `+` global create button >> Track Distance >> Odometer option 3. Enter start and end readings 4. Click the image button and take a start odometer photo 5. Click `Save for later` and verify that the page closes 6. Reopen the Distance Request page and navigate to the `Odometer` tab 7. Verify that the reading and image data entered previously are displayed correctly 8. Clear the readings & delete image so they are empty and click `Save for later` 9. Reopen the Odometer page and verify that no reading data is displayed 10. Re-enter start and end readings and take a start odometer photo 11. Kill the app (Mobile) or close/refresh the site tab (Web) 12. Reopen the app/tab and navigate back to the Odometer page ## Expected Result: Re-entered odometer readings and image data are still displayed correctly (data persistence) after refreshing page or killing/reopening app ## Actual Result: Re-entered odometer readings/image do not persist after refreshing or killing/reopening the page ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e9a5519d-70da-4ea4-95db-2fcd2e11fb1f [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
